description_long: This object is a rectangular radio receiver with a robust, boxy form factor, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century home or office audio equipment. The front panel features two large circular knobs, likely for volume and tuning, accompanied by a row of four smaller buttons or switches to the right. A horizontal rectangular grille, possibly for the speaker, is positioned on the left side of the front face, above a small rectangular display or frequency indicator. The casing appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a smooth, matte finish and minimal decorative elements. The sides and back are plain, with no visible ports or features, emphasizing its utilitarian design. This radio receiver would be commonly found on desks, shelves, or workbenches in urban environments.
description: A rectangular, boxy radio receiver with two large knobs, a speaker grille, and a row of buttons on the front panel. The casing is smooth and matte, with a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large knobs, a speaker grille, a display or indicator, and four small buttons.
description_view_1: Side view showing a plain, featureless panel with the edge of the front knobs visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a completely plain, unadorned surface with no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also plain and featureless, with no controls or openings visible.
